name: PyTorch Multi GPU
description: Multi-GPU PyTorch template for distributed workloads with NCCL-friendly defaults and device topology reporting.

params.script:
import json, torch

device_count = torch.cuda.device_count()
device = 'cuda' if device_count > 0 else 'cpu'
if device_count > 1:
    model = torch.nn.DataParallel(torch.nn.Linear(4096, 4096).to('cuda'))
    x = torch.randn(64, 4096, device='cuda')
    y = model(x)
    throughput = float(y.numel())
else:
    layer = torch.nn.Linear(4096, 4096).to(device)
    x = torch.randn(64, 4096, device=device)
    y = layer(x)
    throughput = float(y.numel())

print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({
  'template': 'pytorch-multi-gpu',
  'device': device,
  'gpu_count': int(device_count),
  'throughput_units': throughput,
  'status': 'completed'
}))

model_cache.behavior: Hot cache pins frequently-used model shards to minimize cross-GPU cold starts.

params.script:
import json, torch

n = torch.cuda.device_count()
device = 'cuda' if n > 0 else 'cpu'
model = torch.nn.Linear(256, 256).to(device)
if n > 1:
    model = torch.nn.DataParallel(model)
x = torch.randn(32, 256, device=device)
y = model(x)
print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({'template':'pytorch-multi-gpu','device':device,'gpu_count':int(n),'throughput_units':int(y.numel()),'status':'completed'}))
